text.title: AWS CLI Setup Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `AWS CLI Setup` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: All developers MUST run AWS CLI v2 (≥ 2.15). v1 is forbidden because it lacks SSO + auto-credential-refresh support.
rule.rationale: v1 SSO support is missing; mixed v1/v2 leads to silently-stale credentials.

rule.statement: Authentication MUST go through IAM Identity Center (SSO) with `aws sso login`; long-lived access keys are forbidden except for service accounts with documented justification.

rationale.source: AWS Identity Center docs; CIS AWS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Long-lived keys are the #1 leaked-credential class; SSO + short-lived STS tokens kill that surface.

rule.statement: Each account MUST have a named profile (`prod`, `staging`, `dev`) in `~/.aws/config`; `default` profile is reserved for the lowest-blast-radius account.
rule.rationale: Unnamed `default` profile leads to wrong-account commands; named profiles force explicit selection.

rule.statement: Console + CLI access MUST require MFA via SSO MFA enforcement or IAM policy condition; bypass exceptions require security-lead sign-off + 30d expiry.

rationale.source: AWS IAM Best Practices; SOC2 CC6.6

rule.rationale: MFA-less roots / IAM users have driven multiple public breaches; enforcement closes the surface.

rule.statement: Plain-text `~/.aws/credentials` access keys are forbidden. Use SSO cached tokens or a credential helper (aws-vault, leapp, granted).
rule.rationale: Plain-text keys committed to git or backed up to iCloud / Dropbox have repeatedly leaked.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
